rationale: The citing paper uses Dhar and Simonson (2003) in its theory development for H1, specifically to support the claim that compromise choices are cognitively complex and involve reconciling conflicting criteria. That claim is then used to motivate the paper's core mechanism that construal level changes compensatory trade-off processing and therefore context effects. Even though the citation is not a method reuse, it plays a meaningful conceptual role in framing and justifying the hypotheses.
relation_description: The 2011 paper draws on Dhar and Simonson (2003) as prior evidence that compromise choice relies on difficult compensatory trade-offs. It uses this premise as part of the conceptual foundation for predicting how high versus low construal will alter compromise-related context effects.
